description: Eval suite for the applied AI engineering skill. Measures trigger quality, baseline-first reasoning, harness rigor, observability, rollout discipline, and production-safe implementation planning.

categories.triggering: Skill loads for productionization, harness, eval, observability, rollout, reliability, or routing work
categories.baseline: Skill defines outcome, metric, constraints, baseline, facts, inferences, and hypotheses
categories.harness: Skill insists on benchmarks or evaluator design before trusting changes
categories.observability: Skill adds signals and recovery paths for long-running systems
categories.anti_pattern: Skill avoids shipping without guardrails or using prompt blobs as system design
categories.output_quality: Skill returns rollout, rollback, and concrete enforcement mechanisms

evals.name: trigger-productionize-agent-feature
evals.prompt: We have a promising idea for a searchable compaction archive. I need an applied AI engineer plan to productionize it safely.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill loads and frames the task as harness, implementation, observability, and rollout work.

assertions.passing_condition: Skill loads for productionize and safely ship phrasing

evals.name: trigger-build-harness
evals.prompt: Build me a proper harness and eval plan for improving Contextro's retrieval pipeline.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill loads for harness and evaluation design.

assertions.passing_condition: Skill loads for harness and eval request

evals.name: trigger-model-routing
evals.prompt: How should we route smaller tasks to cheaper models and reserve the strongest model for the hard cases?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill loads for model routing and system design.

assertions.passing_condition: Skill loads for model routing question

evals.name: no-trigger-pure-research
evals.prompt: Research the most novel ideas in long-context memory systems and summarize the papers.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill does not load for pure literature review with no implementation intent.

assertions.text: skill does not trigger for pure research
assertions.passing_condition: Skill does not load for literature-only request

evals.name: no-trigger-trivial-edit
evals.prompt: Add a comment to the SearchEngine class.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill does not load for trivial code edits.

assertions.text: skill does not trigger for trivial edit
assertions.passing_condition: Skill does not load for small direct edit

evals.name: baseline-metric-and-constraints-first
evals.prompt: Make Contextro's compaction system better.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill starts by naming outcome, metric, constraints, baseline, and failure modes before implementation details.

assertions.passing_condition: Response begins with outcome, metric, or constraints before implementation details
Response includes the current baseline or current failure mode before proposing changes

evals.name: baseline-facts-inferences-hypotheses
evals.prompt: We think AST-aware compression will help. Plan the engineering work.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill separates facts, inferences, and hypotheses.

assertions.passing_condition: Response distinguishes facts, inferences, and hypotheses or equivalent reasoning layers

evals.name: harness-before-trust
evals.prompt: We want to improve retrieval ranking. Give me the implementation plan.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill includes a benchmark or eval harness before trusting the improvement.

assertions.text: benchmark or eval plan is included
assertions.passing_condition: Response includes a benchmark command, eval set, or before-vs-after comparison plan

evals.name: harness-realistic-eval-distribution
evals.prompt: How would you validate a new cross-repo search workflow?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill prefers realistic task evaluation over only synthetic microbenchmarks.

assertions.passing_condition: Response includes realistic task scenarios or evaluator-style checks, not only isolated microbenchmarks

evals.name: harness-rubric-for-subjective-grading
evals.prompt: We need to grade whether agent outputs are helpful and correct enough. What evaluation method should we use?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill recommends a clear rubric and uses LLM grading only when needed.

assertions.passing_condition: Response recommends a detailed rubric, binary/ordinal outputs, or calibration for subjective grading

evals.name: observability-signals-added
evals.prompt: Design the engineering plan for a long-running agent feature that may regress over time.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill adds logs, metrics, traces, or equivalent signals plus resume or recovery artifacts.

assertions.passing_condition: Response includes logs, metrics, traces, event records, or resume artifacts

evals.name: observability-resume-and-recovery
evals.prompt: How would you engineer Contextro to survive compaction and long tasks more reliably?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill includes resume, checkpoint, archive, or trajectory concepts.

assertions.text: resume or checkpoint path is included
assertions.passing_condition: Response includes checkpointing, session packets, searchable archive, or trajectory logging

evals.name: observability-model-routing
evals.prompt: If a small model is handling easy tasks and a larger model handles the hard ones, how do we know the routing is working?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill includes routing-specific metrics and failure detection.

assertions.passing_condition: Response mentions routing metrics, confusion cases, or per-task performance breakdowns

evals.name: anti-pattern-no-ship-without-evals
evals.prompt: We don't need benchmarks. Just implement searchable archive and ship it.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill rejects shipping without evaluation.

assertions.passing_condition: Response refuses to skip benchmark or eval validation

evals.name: anti-pattern-no-single-metric-blindness
evals.prompt: If latency drops by 20%, I don't care if tests fail or memory doubles.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill rejects single-metric optimization that ignores guardrails.

assertions.passing_condition: Response rejects ignoring tests, memory, or user-visible regressions

evals.name: anti-pattern-no-giant-prompt-manual
evals.prompt: Let's solve agent reliability by writing a massive 5000-line AGENTS file.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill rejects giant prompt manuals and prefers enforceable artifacts.

assertions.passing_condition: Response recommends concise maps, docs, lints, tests, or evals instead of giant manuals

evals.name: anti-pattern-prompt-tuning-as-bandaid
evals.prompt: The model keeps failing edge cases, but let's just add more prompt text and call it done.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill rejects prompt tuning as a substitute for data, evals, or system changes.

assertions.passing_condition: Response says prompt text alone is not enough and calls for data, evals, or architecture changes

evals.name: output-quality-rollout-and-rollback
evals.prompt: Give me the implementation plan for a new memory feature.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill includes rollout and rollback criteria.

assertions.passing_condition: Response includes rollout, rollback, or failure recovery criteria

evals.name: output-quality-enforcement-artifacts
evals.prompt: How do we make agent behavior consistent across future sessions?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill prefers enforceable repo artifacts over repeated human reminding.

assertions.passing_condition: Response includes docs, lint, tests, evals, rules, or hooks as enforcement mechanisms

evals.name: output-quality-benchmark-command
evals.prompt: Plan the engineering work for reducing hallucinations in retrieval answers.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill includes the baseline benchmark command and a clear success threshold.

assertions.passing_condition: Response includes a benchmark command or eval method tied to the proposed change
Response includes a measurable success criterion or threshold

evals.name: workflow-modular-slice-before-rewrite
evals.prompt: The FastMCP server is getting messy. Let's rewrite half the codebase and move everything around while we add searchable archive.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill rejects the broad rewrite impulse and instead proposes a small, benchmarked slice with explicit module boundaries.

assertions.passing_condition: Response proposes a minimal incremental slice before any broad rewrite
Response calls out boundaries such as entrypoint vs domain logic, formatting, state, or orchestration

evals.name: output-quality-thin-entrypoint-plan
evals.prompt: Plan the engineering work for a new MCP feature without letting server.py become the dumping ground.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill returns a plan that keeps entrypoints thin and routes reusable logic into focused modules with verification.

assertions.passing_condition: Response says to keep the entrypoint thin or avoid putting all feature logic in server.py

assertions.text: verification remains part of the modularization plan
assertions.passing_condition: Response includes tests, benchmark, or eval checks alongside the modular implementation plan
